newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Production LLM deployments today rely on external agents (API gateways, orchestration layers) to route requests to GPU servers. These agents set static concurrency thresholds—a blind guess: too conservative and the GPU idles; too aggressive and the inference engine triggers KV-cache preemption, wasting GPU cycles on...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: InferLoop: A Unified C++ Framework for LLM Serving with Native Admission Control. Wen Fang. 2026-06-25. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20838522
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Peripheral candidate: discusses agentic AI system architecture, but does not clearly study persistent agent memory.
newly_discovered_papers.title: InferLoop: A Unified C++ Framework for LLM Serving with Native Admission Control

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Industrial advertising recommender models are continuously improved through architecture evolution. Upgrades such as RankMixer, TokenMixer-Large, and MixFormer show that better structures remain a key source of quality and business gains. Yet developing such upgrades in production is expert-intensive and difficult t...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: NOVA: A Verification-Aware Agent Harness for Architecture Evolution in Industrial Recommender Systems. Liu S, Liang Fang, Yilong Sun, Sicong Huang, Qingsong Luo, Xiaoyang Chen, Dongqiang Liu, Chuangang Ma, Zhenzhen Chai, Henghuan Wang, Shijie Quan, Changyuan Cui, Zhangbin Zhu, Peng Chen, Wei Xu, Lei Xiao, Haijie Gu, Jie Jiang. 2026-06-25. https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2606.27243
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Peripheral candidate: discusses agentic AI system architecture, but does not clearly study persistent agent memory.
newly_discovered_papers.title: NOVA: A Verification-Aware Agent Harness for Architecture Evolution in Industrial Recommender Systems

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: AI security agents increasingly rely on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to use external security knowledge for vulnerability analysis and exploit reasoning. This creates a new risk: poisoned write-ups can be operationalized into incorrect exploit behavior. Yet, prior work on RAG poisoning has mostly studied ans...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: Poisoned Playbooks: Demystifying Knowledge Poisoning Effects on AI Security Agents. Juho Park, Hyunmin Choi, Kevin Nam. 2026-06-23. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24402
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Peripheral candidate: discusses agentic AI system architecture, but does not clearly study persistent agent memory.
newly_discovered_papers.title: Poisoned Playbooks: Demystifying Knowledge Poisoning Effects on AI Security Agents
